tasks.description: Create config.lua with setup function, default options, and plantuml executable detection logic.

steps.description: Define configuration schema with java_cmd, plantuml_jar, inkscape_cmd, server_port options
Implement setup function that merges user options with defaults
Implement plantuml executable detection: prefer system plantuml, fallback to java -jar
Add validation for required tools (java/plantuml) with error messages

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Config module exports setup(opts) function that validates and stores configuration. Module detects system plantuml via vim.fn.executable('plantuml') and uses it if available, otherwise uses java -jar plantuml.jar pattern. Missing plantuml/java produces clear error notification.

unit: setup() with empty opts returns default configuration table
setup() with custom opts merges correctly (user opts override defaults)
plantuml_cmd is 'plantuml' when system plantuml is available (mock vim.fn.executable)
plantuml_cmd is 'java -jar /path/to/plantuml.jar' when plantuml_jar is configured
setup() warns when neither plantuml nor java is available

tasks.description: Create util.lua with path manipulation, git root detection, and temp directory management functions.

steps.description: Implement find_git_root(buffer_path) that walks up directory tree to find .git
Implement get_project_folder(buffer_path) returning git root or buffer cwd
Implement ensure_temp_dir() creating /tmp/plantuml.nvim/ if not exists
Implement get_output_dir(buffer_path, subdir) returning umlout/<subdir> path
Implement get_puml_file_info() extracting filename without extension

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Util module exports find_git_root(), get_project_folder(), ensure_temp_dir(), get_output_dir(), get_puml_file_info(). find_git_root returns first parent with .git or nil. Temp directory is created with proper permissions. Output directory path follows project-folder/umlout/<subdir> pattern.

unit: find_git_root('/a/b/.git/c') returns '/a/b'
find_git_root('/a/b/c') with no .git returns nil
get_project_folder('/project/.git/sub/file.puml') returns '/project'
get_project_folder('/no/git/file.puml') returns buffer cwd
ensure_temp_dir() creates /tmp/plantuml.nvim/ with mode 493
get_output_dir('/project/file.puml', 'svg') returns '/project/umlout/svg'

tasks.description: Create executor.lua with async PlantUML and Inkscape command execution wrappers.

steps.description: Implement run_plantuml(input, output_path, format, callback) using vim.system
Implement run_inkscape(svg_path, png_path, dpi, callback) for PNG conversion
Add error handling with vim.notify for command failures
Implement stdout/stderr capture and exit code checking

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Executor module exports run_plantuml() and run_inkscape() async functions. Commands execute via vim.system() with proper argument arrays. Non-zero exit codes trigger vim.notify with ERROR level. Callback receives (success, output_or_error) tuple.

unit: run_plantuml() constructs correct command: plantuml --format svg -o output input
run_plantuml() uses java -jar pattern when configured
run_inkscape() constructs correct command: inkscape --export-dpi=400 --export-filename=out.png in.svg
Command with exit code 0 calls callback(true, output)
Command with exit code 1 calls callback(false, stderr)
integration: run_plantuml() with real plantuml generates valid SVG from test diagram
run_inkscape() with real inkscape converts SVG to PNG

tasks.description: Create creator.lua with PlantumlCreateSVG, PlantumlCreatePNG, and PlantumlCreateUTXT command implementations.

steps.description: Implement create_svg(buffer_path) generating SVG to umlout/svg/
Implement create_png(buffer_path) generating SVG then converting to PNG via Inkscape
Implement create_utxt(buffer_path) generating UTXT to umlout/utxt/
Add success/failure notifications with output file path
Ensure output directories exist before writing

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Creator module exports create_svg(), create_png(), create_utxt() functions. Each function reads current buffer, executes plantuml with correct format, saves to project-folder/umlout/<format>/. PNG conversion chains SVG generation then Inkscape. User receives notification with output path on success or error message on failure.

unit: create_svg() calls executor.run_plantuml with --format svg
create_png() calls run_plantuml then run_inkscape in sequence
create_utxt() calls executor.run_plantuml with --utxt flag
Output directory is created if not exists
Success notification shows full output path
integration: create_svg() with real plantuml creates valid SVG file in umlout/svg/
create_png() creates valid PNG file in umlout/png/
create_utxt() creates valid UTXT file in umlout/utxt/

tasks.description: Create server/server.js as a simple static file server for SVG preview and server.lua for server lifecycle management.

steps.description: Create server.js using Node.js http module serving /tmp/plantuml.nvim/ directory
Add CORS headers for local development
Implement server.lua with start_server() and stop_server() functions
Track server process with vim.loop for lifecycle management
Add port conflict detection with fallback to next available port

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Server module exports start_server(callback) and stop_server(). Node.js server serves files from /tmp/plantuml.nvim/ on configured port (default 8080). Server process is tracked and can be stopped cleanly. Port conflicts trigger automatic port increment. Server URL is passed to callback on successful start.

unit: server.js returns 200 for existing file in temp directory
server.js returns 404 for non-existent file
server.js includes CORS headers in response
start_server() spawns node process with correct arguments
stop_server() kills the server process
integration: Server serves SVG file that was generated by create_svg()
Browser can access the SVG via http://localhost:8080/file.svg
e2e-manual: Open browser to server URL and verify SVG renders correctly

tasks.description: Create preview.lua with PlantumlPreviewUTXT command implementation including smart window management and auto-refresh on save.

steps.description: Implement preview_utxt() generating temp UTXT and opening in split window
Implement find_preview_window() checking for existing preview buffer
Implement update_preview() reusing existing window or creating new split
Register BufWritePost autocmd for .puml/.uml files to trigger auto-refresh
Implement cleanup function to remove autocmd when preview closes

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Preview module exports preview_utxt() function. First call creates vertical split to the right with UTXT content. Subsequent calls update the existing buffer without creating new windows. BufWritePost autocmd triggers automatic preview refresh for .puml/.uml files. Buffer variable 'plantuml_preview' marks preview buffers.

unit: find_preview_window() returns nil when no preview exists
find_preview_window() returns window id when preview buffer exists
preview_utxt() creates new split when no preview exists
preview_utxt() reuses existing window when preview is open
Autocmd is registered with correct pattern (*.puml,*.uml)
Autocmd is removed when preview buffer is wiped
integration: preview_utxt() displays real plantuml UTXT output from test diagram
Saving .puml file triggers preview buffer update
e2e-manual: Verify UTXT preview displays Unicode characters correctly

tasks.description: Implement PlantumlPreview command that generates SVG to temp directory, starts server if needed, and opens browser.

steps.description: Implement preview_svg() generating SVG to /tmp/plantuml.nvim/
Start preview server if not already running
Use vim.ui.open() to open browser with server URL + filename
Track server state to avoid multiple server instances

tasks.acceptance-criteria: preview_svg() generates SVG file to temp directory. Server is started lazily on first preview call. Browser opens via vim.ui.open() pointing to localhost:port/filename.svg. Multiple preview calls reuse the same server instance. Server is stopped on Neovim exit via VimLeave autocmd.

unit: preview_svg() generates SVG with unique temp filename
Server is not started if already running
vim.ui.open() is called with correct URL format
VimLeave autocmd stops the server
integration: Full preview flow: generate SVG, start server, open browser
Browser displays the generated SVG diagram
e2e-manual: Verify browser opens automatically with SVG preview
Verify SVG renders correctly in browser

tasks.task: Register user commands and module entry
tasks.description: Create plugin/plantuml.lua with command registration and lua/plantuml/init.lua with public API exports.

steps.description: Create init.lua exporting setup(), preview(), preview_utxt(), create_svg(), create_png(), create_utxt()
Create plugin/plantuml.lua with nvim_create_user_command for all 5 commands
Add command completion for file arguments if applicable
Add VimLeave autocmd to cleanup server and temp files

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Plugin registers 5 Neovim user commands: PlantumlPreview, PlantumlPreviewUTXT, PlantumlCreateSVG, PlantumlCreatePNG, PlantumlCreateUTXT. Commands work on current buffer .puml/.uml files. setup() function is called on plugin load with optional user configuration. VimLeave autocmd ensures server is stopped.

unit: :PlantumlPreview command exists and is callable
:PlantumlPreviewUTXT command exists and is callable
:PlantumlCreateSVG command exists and is callable
:PlantumlCreatePNG command exists and is callable
:PlantumlCreateUTXT command exists and is callable
setup() merges user config with defaults
integration: Full workflow: edit .puml file, run :PlantumlPreview, see browser output
Full workflow: edit .puml file, run :PlantumlCreateSVG, verify file exists
e2e-manual: Install plugin, run :PlantumlPreview on sample diagram, verify browser opens
Run :PlantumlPreviewUTXT, edit and save file, verify preview updates
